title: spergula rubra subsp. nicaeensis
intro: Spergula rubra subsp. nicaeensis, also known as the red spurry or red sparrows, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Spergula rubra subsp. nicaeensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
